2603.26031.title: Designing Fatigue-Aware VR Interfaces via Biomechanical Models
2603.26031.paper_summary: Prolonged mid-air interaction in virtual reality (VR) causes arm fatigue and discomfort, negatively affecting user experience. Incorporating ergonomic considerations into VR user interface (UI) design typically requires extensive human-in-the-loop evaluation. Although biomechanical models have been used to simulate human behavior in HCI tasks, their application as surrogate users for ergonomic VR UI design remains underexplored. We propose a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework that leverages biomechanical user models to evaluate and optimize VR interfaces for mid-air interaction. A motion agent is trained to perform button-press tasks in VR under sequential conditions, using realistic movement strategies and estimating muscle-level effort via a validated three-compartment control with recovery (3CC-r) fatigue model. The simulated fatigue output serves as feedback for a UI agent that optimizes UI element layout via reinforcement learning (RL) to minimize fatigue. We compare the RL-optimized layout against a manually-designed centered baseline and a Bayesian optimized baseline. Results show that fatigue trends from the biomechanical model align with human user data. Moreover, the RL-optimized layout using simulated fatigue feedback produced significantly lower perceived fatigue in a follow-up human study. We further demonstrate the framework's extensibility via a simulated case study on longer sequential tasks with non-uniform interaction frequencies. To our knowledge, this is the first work using simulated biomechanical muscle fatigue as a direct optimization signal for VR UI layout design. Our findings highlight the potential of biomechanical user models as effective surrogate tools for ergonomic VR interface design, enabling efficient early-stage iteration with less reliance on extensive human participation.
2603.26031.paper_summary_zh: 長時間在虛擬實境 (VR) 中進行空中互動會導致手臂疲勞和不適，對使用者體驗產生負面影響。將人體工學考量納入 VR 使用者介面 (UI) 設計通常需要廣泛的人機互動評估。雖然生物力學模型已被用來模擬人類在 HCI 任務中的行為，但作為人體工學 VR UI 設計的替代使用者，其應用仍未被充分探索。我們提出了一個層級強化學習框架，利用生物力學使用者模型來評估和優化 VR 介面以進行空中互動。一個動作代理被訓練在 VR 中在連續條件下執行按鈕按壓任務，使用真實的運動策略並通過經驗驗證的三室控制與恢復 (3CC-r) 疲勞模型來估算肌肉層級的努力。模擬的疲勞輸出作為 UI 代理的反饋，該代理通過強化學習 (RL) 優化 UI 元素佈局以最小化疲勞。我們將 RL 優化的佈局與手動設計的中心基準和貝葉斯優化基準進行比較。結果顯示，生物力學模型的疲勞趨勢與人類使用者數據一致。此外，使用模擬疲勞反饋的 RL 優化佈局在後續的人類研究中顯著降低了感知疲勞。我們進一步通過模擬案例研究展示該框架的可擴展性，該案例涉及具有非均勻互動頻率的更長連續任務。據我們所知，這是第一個使用模擬生物力學肌肉疲勞作為 VR UI 佈局設計的直接優化信號的研究。我們的研究結果突顯了生物力學使用者模型作為有效的替代工具在人體工學 VR 介面設計中的潛力，能夠在早期階段進行高效的迭代，並減少對廣泛人力參與的依賴。
2603.26031.author: Harshitha Voleti et.al.
